# Cross-Platform IDE Plugin Development - Discovery Document

> Research conducted: January 2026
> Topic: Building plugins that work across multiple IDEs (VSCode, JetBrains, Zed)

## Executive Summary

**There is no unified framework like "React Native for IDE plugins"** that allows writing a single codebase deployable to all editors. However, **standardized protocols** exist that enable sharing 60-80% of core logic across editors through thin adapter layers.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Cross-Platform Protocols](#cross-platform-protocols)
2. [Editor-Specific Architectures](#editor-specific-architectures)
3. [Agent Client Protocol (ACP) - Deep Dive](#agent-client-protocol-acp---deep-dive)
4. [Recommended Architecture](#recommended-architecture)
5. [Comparison Matrix](#comparison-matrix)
6. [Resources & Links](#resources--links)

---

## Cross-Platform Protocols

### 1. Language Server Protocol (LSP) - The Gold Standard

**The de facto standard for cross-editor language features.**

| Attribute | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| Created by | Microsoft |
| Current Version | 3.17 |
| Transport | JSON-RPC 2.0 |
| Support | VSCode, JetBrains (via LSP4IJ), Zed, Neovim, Emacs, Sublime, Eclipse, Visual Studio |

#### Features Covered by LSP

- **Code Intelligence**
  - Autocomplete / IntelliSense
  - Go to Definition / Declaration / Implementation
  - Find All References
  - Hover documentation
  - Signature Help

- **Diagnostics**
  - Errors, warnings, hints
  - Real-time validation

- **Code Manipulation**
  - Code Actions / Quick Fixes
  - Rename / Refactor
  - Formatting (document & range)
  - Organize imports

- **Navigation**
  - Document symbols
  - Workspace symbols
  - Folding ranges
  - Breadcrumbs

#### LSP Architecture

```
┌─────────────────┐                      ┌──────────────────┐
│   VSCode        │◄────── JSON-RPC ────►│                  │
├─────────────────┤                      │                  │
│   JetBrains     │◄────── JSON-RPC ────►│  Language Server │
├─────────────────┤                      │   (your code)    │
│   Zed           │◄────── JSON-RPC ────►│                  │
├─────────────────┤                      │                  │
│   Neovim        │◄────── JSON-RPC ────►│                  │
└─────────────────┘                      └──────────────────┘
```

#### Key Insight: LSP Does NOT Handle UI

LSP is purely a data protocol. It tells the editor "there's an error on line 42" but the editor decides how to render it (red underline, icon, tooltip style, etc.).

#### Implementation Languages

Language Servers can be written in any language:
- **TypeScript/JavaScript** - Most common, great tooling
- **Rust** - High performance (rust-analyzer, etc.)
- **Go** - gopls
- **Python** - pylsp
- **C#** - OmniSharp

---

### 2. Debug Adapter Protocol (DAP)

**The LSP equivalent for debugging.**

| Attribute | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| Created by | Microsoft |
| Transport | JSON-RPC |
| Support | VSCode, JetBrains (via LSP4IJ), Zed, Neovim, Eclipse |

#### Features Covered

- Breakpoints (line, conditional, function, data)
- Step execution (into, over, out)
- Variable inspection
- Call stack navigation
- Watch expressions
- Debug console / REPL

#### Architecture

```
┌─────────────┐     DAP      ┌─────────────────┐     Native     ┌──────────┐
│   Editor    │◄────────────►│  Debug Adapter  │◄──────────────►│ Debugger │
└─────────────┘              └─────────────────┘                └──────────┘
```

---

### 3. Model Context Protocol (MCP) - AI Integration

**Standard for AI tools to integrate with editors and access context.**

| Attribute | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| Created by | Anthropic (November 2024) |
| Adopted by | OpenAI (March 2025), Zed, IBM BeeAI, Microsoft Copilot Studio, Windsurf |
| Governance | Donated to Linux Foundation (December 2025) via AAIF |
| Transport | JSON-RPC 2.0 (LSP-inspired) |

#### Features Covered

- **Resources** - File access, project context
- **Tools** - Function execution, code manipulation
- **Prompts** - Custom prompt templates
- **Sampling** - LLM interaction patterns

#### MCP Does NOT Handle UI

Like LSP, MCP is a data/action protocol. The editor/client decides how to present information to users.

---

### 4. Agent Client Protocol (ACP) - Emerging Standard

**Protocol for AI coding agents to work across multiple IDEs.**

| Attribute | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| Created by | Zed + JetBrains (collaboration, October 2025) |
| Status | Active development |
| Current Support | Zed (native), Neovim (plugins), Marimo, JetBrains (coming soon) |
| VSCode | Uncertain (under discussion by Microsoft) |

#### Key Differentiator: ACP Has UI Semantics

Unlike LSP/MCP, ACP defines **UI interaction patterns** for coding agents:

| UI Feature | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| Multi-file diffs | Standard way to present changes across files |
| Live progress | Streaming updates as agent works |
| Agent following | Editor follows agent through codebase (shows what's being read/edited) |
| Rich Markdown | All human-readable text defaults to Markdown |
| Approval workflows | Standard patterns for approve/reject actions |

#### However: Rendering is Still Editor-Specific

ACP defines "what" to show, not "how" to render it:
```
ACP: "Show a 3-file diff for user approval"
Editor: Decides layout (side-by-side, inline), colors, fonts, etc.
```

#### Compatible Agents

- Claude Code
- Codex CLI
- Gemini
- Goose (by Block)
- StackPack

---

## Editor-Specific Architectures

### JetBrains IDEs (IntelliJ Platform)

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| Languages | Kotlin (preferred), Java |
| Build System | Gradle + IntelliJ Platform Gradle Plugin 2.x |
| Runtime | JVM |
| Distribution | JetBrains Marketplace |

#### 2025.3 Changes

- IntelliJ IDEA Community and Ultimate unified into single distribution
- Use `intellijIdea()` dependency helper instead of `intellijIdeaCommunity()`
- IC builds no longer available for 2025.3+
- Plugin DevKit enhanced with Kotlin Notebook integration

#### Using LSP in JetBrains

Two approaches:
1. **LSP4IJ** - Open source LSP client by Red Hat (recommended)
2. **Native API** - Use IntelliJ Platform's built-in LSP support (limited)

#### Key APIs

- **PSI** (Program Structure Interface) - AST representation
- **UAST** (Unified AST) - Cross-language abstraction for JVM languages
- **VFS** (Virtual File System) - File access abstraction
- **Actions** - Commands and menu items
- **Tool Windows** - Custom panels

---

### VSCode

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| Languages | TypeScript, JavaScript |
| Build System | npm/yarn + webpack/esbuild |
| Runtime | Node.js (extension host process) |
| Distribution | VSCode Marketplace, Open VSX |

#### Extension Capabilities

- **Language Features** - Native LSP client support
- **Webviews** - Custom HTML/CSS/JS panels (sandboxed iframes)
- **Tree Views** - Sidebar panels with tree structures
- **Commands** - Keyboard shortcuts, command palette
- **Debug Adapters** - Native DAP support
- **Tasks** - Build/run configurations

#### Important Note (2025)

The Webview UI Toolkit for VS Code was deprecated January 1, 2025. Extensions needing custom UI should use standard web technologies or consider alternatives.

---

### Zed

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| Languages | Rust (compiled to WebAssembly) |
| Build System | Cargo + wasm32-wasip1 target |
| Runtime | Wasmtime (sandboxed) |
| Distribution | Zed Extension Registry |

#### Why WebAssembly?

- Cross-platform without recompilation
- Sandboxed execution (can't crash editor)
- Fast execution
- Single artifact for all platforms

#### Current Capabilities

- Language support (via Tree-sitter + LSP)
- Themes and icon themes
- Slash commands
- MCP servers
- Debugger protocols (DAP)

#### Current Limitations

- No custom UI panels
- No arbitrary HTTP requests
- Limited filesystem access
- No modification of editor chrome

---

## Agent Client Protocol (ACP) - Deep Dive

### Why ACP Matters

Before ACP, agent developers had to:
1. Build VSCode extension
2. Build JetBrains plugin
3. Build Zed extension
4. Build Neovim plugin
5. Or rely on CLI-only interface

ACP provides a **universal integration point** - implement ACP once, work in all compatible editors.

### Protocol Flow

```
┌─────────────────┐                         ┌─────────────────┐
│                 │  1. Initialize          │                 │
│     Editor      │────────────────────────►│     Agent       │
│   (ACP Client)  │                         │  (ACP Server)   │
│                 │  2. Capabilities        │                 │
│                 │◄────────────────────────│                 │
│                 │                         │                 │
│                 │  3. Context/Files       │                 │
│                 │────────────────────────►│                 │
│                 │                         │                 │
│                 │  4. Proposed Changes    │                 │
│                 │◄────────────────────────│                 │
│                 │                         │                 │
│                 │  5. User Approval       │                 │
│                 │────────────────────────►│                 │
│                 │                         │                 │
│                 │  6. Apply Changes       │                 │
│                 │◄────────────────────────│                 │
└─────────────────┘                         └─────────────────┘
```

### Key Design Principles

1. **Editor-Agnostic Agents** - Agents work anywhere ACP is supported
2. **No Vendor Lock-in** - Switch agents without switching editors
3. **Confidence & Control** - Users see exactly what agents plan to do
4. **Great UX** - Streaming updates, clear intent, native feel

---

## Recommended Architecture

### Multi-Layer Strategy

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                       Core Logic Layer                          │
│              (TypeScript or Rust - business logic)              │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐ │
│  │   LSP Server    │  │   DAP Server    │  │   MCP Server    │ │
│  │  (lang features)│  │   (debugging)   │  │  (AI context)   │ │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘ │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
             ┌────────────────┼────────────────┐
             ▼                ▼                ▼
    ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐
    │    VSCode    │  │   JetBrains  │  │     Zed      │
    │   Extension  │  │    Plugin    │  │   Extension  │
    │              │  │              │  │              │
    │  - Spawns    │  │  - Uses      │  │  - WASM      │
    │    servers   │  │    LSP4IJ    │  │    wrapper   │
    │  - UI config │  │  - UI config │  │  - Config    │
    └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘
```

### Code Sharing Breakdown

| Layer | Shared | Editor-Specific |
|-------|--------|-----------------|
| Language Logic | 100% | 0% |
| LSP/DAP Servers | 100% | 0% |
| Server Spawning | 0% | 100% |
| Configuration UI | 0% | 100% |
| Custom Panels | 0% | 100% |
| Commands/Actions | ~50% | ~50% |

**Realistic code sharing: 60-80%** when using LSP/DAP/MCP properly.

---

## Comparison Matrix

### Cross-Platform Frameworks vs IDE Plugins

| Aspect | React Native / Tauri | IDE Plugins |
|--------|---------------------|-------------|
| Model | Unified framework | Protocols + adapters |
| Code Sharing | 90%+ | 60-80% |
| UI | Component abstraction | Editor-specific |
| Build | Single toolchain | Multiple toolchains |
| Deployment | One artifact | Per-editor artifacts |
| Learning Curve | Learn framework once | Learn each ecosystem |

### Why No "React Native for IDEs" Exists

1. **Vastly Different UI APIs** - Each editor has unique UI paradigms
2. **Deep Integration Needs** - Plugins need access to editor internals
3. **Established Ecosystems** - Marketplaces, communities already exist
4. **Security Models Vary** - Sandboxing, permissions differ greatly
5. **Performance Requirements** - Editors are performance-critical

---

## Resources & Links

### Official Protocol Documentation

| Protocol | Official Site | Specification |
|----------|---------------|---------------|
| LSP | [microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/) | [3.17 Spec](https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/) |
| DAP | [microsoft.github.io/debug-adapter-protocol](https://microsoft.github.io/debug-adapter-protocol/) | [Spec](https://microsoft.github.io/debug-adapter-protocol/specification) |
| MCP | [modelcontextprotocol.io](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) | [2025-11-25](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25) |
| ACP | [zed.dev/acp](https://zed.dev/acp) | [GitHub](https://github.com/agentclientprotocol/agent-client-protocol) |

### Editor Development Documentation

| JetBrains | [IntelliJ Platform SDK](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/developing-plugins.html) | [Quick Start](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/docs/intellij/plugins-quick-start.html) |
| VSCode | [Extension API](https://code.visualstudio.com/api) | [Your First Extension](https://code.visualstudio.com/api/get-started/your-first-extension) |
| Zed | [Extension Docs](https://zed.dev/docs/extensions/developing-extensions) | [Extensions Blog](https://zed.dev/blog/zed-decoded-extensions) |

### Key Libraries & Tools

| Tool | Purpose | Link |
|------|---------|------|
| LSP4IJ | LSP client for JetBrains | [GitHub](https://github.com/redhat-developer/lsp4ij) |
| vscode-languageclient | LSP client for VSCode | [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/vscode-languageclient) |
| tower-lsp | LSP server framework (Rust) | [crates.io](https://crates.io/crates/tower-lsp) |
| vscode-languageserver | LSP server framework (TS) | [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/vscode-languageserver) |

### Announcements & Blog Posts

- [JetBrains × Zed: ACP Announcement](https://blog.jetbrains.com/ai/2025/10/jetbrains-zed-open-interoperability-for-ai-coding-agents-in-your-ide/)
- [IntelliJ Platform 2025.3 Changes](https://blog.jetbrains.com/platform/2025/11/intellij-platform-2025-3-what-plugin-developers-should-know/)
- [Zed Extensions Deep Dive](https://zed.dev/blog/zed-decoded-extensions)
- [Anthropic MCP Introduction](https://www.anthropic.com/news/model-context-protocol)
- [ACP Introduction by Block/Goose](https://block.github.io/goose/blog/2025/10/24/intro-to-agent-client-protocol-acp/)

---

## Conclusion

### Decision Framework

| If you need... | Use... |
|----------------|--------|
| Language features (autocomplete, diagnostics) | **LSP** |
| Debugging | **DAP** |
| AI tool integration | **MCP** |
| AI agent integration with UI | **ACP** |
| Custom UI panels | Editor-specific APIs |

### Recommended Approach

1. **Identify core functionality** - What does your plugin do?
2. **Map to protocols** - Which protocols cover your needs?
3. **Choose implementation language** - TypeScript (broad support) or Rust (performance + Zed native)
4. **Build core servers** - LSP, DAP, MCP as needed
5. **Create thin adapters** - Minimal editor-specific code

The investment in protocol-based architecture pays off significantly when targeting multiple editors.
